findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:309-337 contains one continuous embodied casualty beat (touch + visible injury detail + immediate decision consequence for Ina) before any summary montage; FAIL if harm remains primarily reported through screens or generalized crowd description.
findings.problem: Sanitization/timidity drift at the violence apex: once mass harm begins, the prose pulls back to monitor-view and montage snapshots instead of staying in embodied, scene-level consequence. That distance softens the chapter's central moral damage right when stakes peak.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the riot escalation around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:309-337 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:603-604 so Ina directly encounters one injured person in real space (not only via feed), takes a concrete action, and absorbs a physical/emotional cost before the scene returns to command decisions.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited spans contain no standalone narration fragments of the form single noun/phrase sentence (for example, 'Negotiators.') and each sentence has an explicit grammatical subject; FAIL if fragment-stacking remains in those spans.
findings.problem: Elliptical clipping appears repeatedly in narration (subjectless/telegraphic fragments), which violates constitution guidance and creates an AI-staccato rhythm that reads as emphasis shortcut rather than crafted compression.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework clipped narration at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:179-180, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:253-256, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:351-352 into grammatically complete sentences with explicit subjects/articles while preserving urgency through syntax and cadence, not omission.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, across the cited Ina/Minjae lines, at least four of five non-legal utterances use contractions and any remaining full-form line is contextually justified on-page (formal quoting, legal precision, or deliberate emphasis); FAIL if uncontracted speech remains the default texture in those beats.
findings.problem: Dialogue register drifts stiff for high-contraction characters (Ina, Minjae) during high-pressure beats via repeated uncontracted forms ('does not', 'are not', 'did not', 'it is'). The book default is high contraction use, so this reads over-formal unless intentionally marked.
findings.rewrite_direction: Adjust high-pressure lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:89, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:125, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:403, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:523-527, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:469 to contracted speech by default; keep full forms only where deliberate precision/formality is explicitly motivated in the surrounding beat.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised chapter contains no more than two 'There was/there it was' pivot formulas and the three cited beats no longer share the same opening construction; FAIL if all three still hinge on that repeated sentence shape.
findings.problem: A recurring rhetorical construction ('There was/There it was...') appears three times and becomes a prose tic, flattening turns that should land with distinct energy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the pivot lines at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:59, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:99, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:155 so each beat enters through different syntax and scene-specific action or image rather than repeating the same declarative turn formula.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the cited passage contains no more than three explicit 'left hand' stress mentions and at least two replacement cues produce distinct behavioral outcomes in-scene; FAIL if the same hand-tremor phrasing remains the dominant stress signal throughout.
findings.problem: Mechanical repetition of Ji-eun's stress tell ('left hand' tremor) reads like over-literal reuse of style-bible markers rather than evolving characterization, reducing tonal range in high-intensity scenes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the tremor motif but vary expression across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:189-557: replace at least two repeated 'left hand shaking/tremor' mentions with different pressure markers (breath disruption, vocal break, jaw lock, dissociation, motor error) that also alter scene action.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised exfil span shows at least one explicit, named containment response and one irreversible cost (lost route, identified vehicle, injured ally, burned asset) before the van clears danger; FAIL if escape still resolves after only local scuffle pressure.
findings.problem: Declared systemic stakes (kidnapping/national-security exposure) are partially undercut by a comparatively light escape corridor: after explicit containment threats, exfiltration faces only a brief two-guard friction beat and then no immediate pursuit.
findings.rewrite_direction: Strengthen causal resistance in snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_09.md:471-510 by adding one concrete institutional containment action triggered by Kwon's order (checkpoint alert, plate capture, dispatch call, staff lockout) and forcing Ina's team to pay an immediate operational cost to evade it.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the beat at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3858-3861 keeps the defiance but avoids "went still/flat" phrasing and reads as Ji-eun-specific performance language. Fail if it remains generic face-freeze diction.
findings.problem: Recurring full-book pattern (5 chapters: 07, 09, 10, 13, 17): default facial-freeze construction. This chapter's instance should be REPLACED.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3858-3861 so Ji-eun's refusal is rendered through a more specific behavioral cue (voice texture, breath pattern, hand tremor control) instead of "mouth went flat."

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised spans at snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3486-3490 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3734-3742 maintain threat but read as event-specific language rather than repeated institutional mantra. Fail if the same token loop dominates both beats.
findings.problem: Style-token overfitting pattern (recurs across chapters 05, 09, 17): repeated abstraction tokens (signal/continuity/discipline) begin to sound like carried-over prompt vocabulary rather than scene-discovered language.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3486-3490 and snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3734-3742 so Kwon's control argument lands through immediate stage-control facts and consequences, not repeated slogan nouns.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
